content.full_text:
City of Dorris
P.O. Box 768
Dorris, California 96023
916-397-3511


FEBRUARY 3, 1992

TO: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
    428 J STREET SUITE 800
    SACRAMENTO CA 95814

ATTEN: DE'ANNA STONE

SUBJECT: RELEASE OF CONFLICT OF INTEREST FOR CITIZEN ADVISORY COMMITTEE
         MEMBER FOR THE CITY OF DORRIS.

DUE TO THE FACT, WAYNE BAY HAD APPLIED FOR A LOAN THROUGH C.H.R.P.O.
PROGRAM. DUE TO THE FACT COMPARABLES WERE UNAVAILABLE FOR THE CITY
OF DORRIS, THE C.H.R.P.O. LOAN COULD NOT PROCEED.
DUE TO WITHDRAWALS AT THE CLOSE OUT OF THE C.D.B.G. 1989, IT WAS
NECESSARY TO COMMIT THIS MONEY IMMEDIATELY. WAYNE BAY'S BID SHEETS WERE
ALREADY ON FILE AND THE CITIZEN ADVISORY COMMITTEE CHOSE TO PICK
BAY'S LOAN THROUGH COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT BLOCK GRANT OTHER THAN LOSE
THE MONEY. WAYNE BAY HAS ABSTAINED FROM ATTENDING LOAN COMMITTEE
MEETINGS REGARDING HIS LOAN AND HAS ABSTAINED FROM ANY VOTING OR
DISCUSSION REGARDING HIS LOAN. WAYNE BAY AGREES NOT TO PARTICIPATE
IN ANY REVIEWS OF HIS LOAN.
WE HEREBY RELEASE WAYNE BAY OF ANY CONFLICT REGARDING THE COMMUNITY
DEVELOPMENT BLOCK GRANT HE HAS BEEN AWARDED.




SIGNED:

___________________________ MAYOR, ROT THIEL

___________________________ COUNCILWOMAN, MARJORIE HAZELWOOD

___________________________ COUNCILMAN, JOHNNY CLEM

WRAY E. KIRSHER (Retired)
ROBERT D. WINSTON
ROBERT B. BOSTON

Legal Assistants:
GEORGIA I. TOMS
SALLY A. GAUTREAUX

KIRSHER, WINSTON & BOSTON
A LAW CORPORATION

219 Main Street, P.O. Box 669
Weed, CA 96094 (916) 938-5438
FAX (916) 938-3247

205 N. Mt. Shasta Blvd., Suite 400, P.O. Box 177
Mt. Shasta, CA 96067 (916) 926-3444
FAX (916) 926-3599

3 February 1992

City Council
City of Dorris
P.O. Box 768
Dorris, CA 96023

Dear Councilmembers:

This is in response to the request for a conflict-of-interest
opinion regarding Councilmember Warren Krouse and his
involvement in the City's Community Development Block Grant. I
understand that he will be bidding on construction jobs which
will be funded by the CDBG.

In my opinion, Mr. Krouse has no conflict by reason of his
position as a councilmember which would prevent him from bidding
on jobs which will be funded by the CDBG. This is because he
will exercise no discretionary or decision-making functions in
connection with the consideration of the CDBG construction bids,
that being within the exclusive authority of the City's Block
Grant citizen advisory committee.

I understand that, in addition, Councilmember Krouse will
abstain from any Council decisions in regard to the CDBG.

The Council may feel free to share this opinion with any
interested persons. Please be advised, however, that my opinion
does not relieve Councilmember Krouse from liability under
conflict of interest laws; only a formal opinion from the Fair
Political Practices Commission has that effect.

Sincerely,

Robert D. Winston

RDW/mm

THE CITIZEN ADVISORY LOAN COMMITTEE
FOR THE CITY OF DORRIS

TO:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J STREET SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

RE: CONFLICT OF INTEREST RELEASE FOR WARREN KROUSE, DORRIS
CITY COUNCILMEMBER

WE THE CITIZEN ADVISORY LOAN COMMITTEE, FOR THE CITY OF DORRIS,
ARE RELEASING WARREN KROUSE FROM ANY CONFLICT OF INTEREST REGARDING
THE C.D.B.G. PROGRAM, ON THE ADVISE FROM GREAT NORTHERN CORPORATION.
DUE TO THE FACT THAT WARREN KROUSE'S SOLE LIVELYHOOD IS HIS CONTRACTOR
BUSINESS, WARREN KROUSE WILL ABSTAIN FROM DISCUSSION AND VOTING
ON ANY BUSINESS REGARDING THE C.D.B.G. PROGRAM.

ON THIS _____ 14 DAY OF __________ 1992
[illegible]

WAYNE BAY, CHAIRMAN

[illegible]
ROBERT HENSLEY, BOARD MEMBER

[illegible]
JAMES ALSTON, BOARD MEMBER

_________________________________
ROSEMARY HUNICUTT, BOARD MEMBER

[illegible]
PAULINE FAIRCHILD BLOOD, BOARD MEMBER

City of Dorris
P.O. Box 768
Dorris, California 96023
916-397-3511

FEBRUARY 3, 1992

TO: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
    428 J STREET SUITE 800
    SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

ATTEN: DE'ANNA STONE

SUBJECT: RELEASE OF CONFLICT OF INTEREST FOR CITY COUNCIL MEMBER FOR
         THE CITY OF DORRIS


DUE TO THE FACT THAT WARREN KROUSE/CITY COUNCIL MEMBER IS NOT INVOLVED
WITH ANY LOAN DECISIONS OR BID AWARDS FOR THE COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT
BLOCK GRANT. DUE TO THE FACT THAT WARREN KROUSE AGREES TO ABSTAIN FROM ANY
COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT BLOCK GRANT FINANCIAL TRANSACTION DECISIONS.
DUE TO THE FACT THAT WARREN KROUSE'S SOLE LIVELIHOOD IS HIS CONTRACTORS
BUSINESS. WE HEREBY AGREE TO RELEASE WARREN KROUSE OF ANY CONFLICT
REGARDING THE COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT BLOCK GRANT.
WARREN KROUSE AGREES NOT TO VOTE OR DISCUSS OR PARTICIPATE IN THE
C.D.B.G. DECISIONS .


SIGNED:

[signature]           MAYOR, ROY THIEL

[signature]           COUNCILWOMAN, MARJORIE HAZELWOOD

[signature]           COUNCILMAN, JOHNNY CLEM

WRAY E. KIRSHER (Retired)
ROBERT D. WINSTON
ROBERT B. BOSTON

Legal Assistants:
GEORGIA L. TOMS
SALLY A. GAUTREAUX

KIRSHER, WINSTON & BOSTON
A LAW CORPORATION

[X] 219 Main Street, P.O. Box 669
Weed, CA 96094 (916)938-5458
FAX (916) 938-3247

□ 205 N. Mt. Shasta Blvd., Suite 400, P.O. Box 177
Mt. Shasta, CA 96067 (916)926-3444
FAX (916) 926-3599

3 February 1992


City Council
City of Dorris
P.O. Box 768
Dorris, CA 96023

Dear Councilmembers:

This is in response to the request for a conflict-of-interest
opinion regarding City Treasurer Tami Tripp and her involvement
in the City's Community Development Block Grant. I understand
that she has applied for a grant under the City's program.

In my opinion, Ms. Tripp has no conflict by reason of her
position as City Treasurer. This is because she exercises no
discretionary or decision-making functions in connection with
the planning or implementation of the CDBG program.

Under state law, a city treasurer must disburse funds upon the
lawful authorization of the city council.

Furthermore, I understand that Ms. Tripp will not deal with any
audits of disbursements on her grant, and that those will be
assigned to other staff members.

The Council may feel free to share this opinion with any
interested persons. Please be advised, however, that my opinion
does not relieve Ms. Tripp from liability under conflict of
interest laws; only a formal opinion from the Fair Political
Practices Commission has that effect.

Sincerely,

Robert D. Winston

RDW/mm

THE CITIZEN ADVISORY LOAN COMMITTEE
FOR THE CITY OF DORRIS

TO:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J STREET SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

RE: CONFLICT OF INTEREST RELEASE FOR TAMI TRIPP, DORRIS CITY
TREASURER

ATT: DEANNA STONE

WE THE CITIZEN ADVISORY LOAN COMMITTEE, FOR THE CITY OF DORRIS, ARE
RELEASING TAMI TRIPP FROM ANY CONFLICT OF INTEREST REGARDING THE
CDBG PROGRAM, ON THE ADVISE FROM GREAT NORTHERN CORPORATION.
DUE TO THE FACT THAT TAMI TRIPP HAD APPLIED FOR A CDBG LOAN TWO
YEARS PREVIOUS TO HER EMPLOYMENT WITH THE CITY OF DORRIS.

ON THIS 14 DAY OF February 1992

[signature]
WAYNE BAY, CHAIRMAN

[signature]
ROBERT HENSLEY, BOARD MEMBER

[signature]
JAMES ALSTON, BOARD MEMBER

[signature]
ROSEMARY HUNICUT, BOARD MEMBER

[signature]
PAULINE FAIRCHILD BLOOD, BOARD MEMBER

City of Dorris
P.O. Box 768
Dorris, California 96023
916-397-3511


JANUARY 3, 1992

TO: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
    428 J STREET, SUITE 800
    SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

ATTEN: DE'ANNA STONE

SUBJECT: RELEASE OF CONFLICT OF INTEREST FOR EMPLOYEE/APPOINTED TREASURER
         OF THE CITY OF DORRIS

DUE TO THE FACT, TAMI TRIPP HAD APPLIED TWO YEARS PRIOR TO HER
EMPLOYMENT AND APPOINTMENT AS TREASURER OF THE CITY OF DORRIS FOR
THE COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT BLOCK GRANT.TAMI TRIPP AGREES NOT TO RECEIPT
OR DISBURSE ANY MONIES CONCERNING HER LOAN.
WE HEREBY AGREE TO RELEASE TAMI TRIPP OF ANY CONFLICT REGARDING
THE COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT BLOCK GRANT.


SIGNED:

[signature] __________________ MAYOR, ROY THIEL

[signature] __________________ COUNCILWOMAN, MARJORIE HAZELWOOD

[signature] __________________ COUNCILMAN, JOHNNY CLEM
